archetypes.name: Interruptible Cast
archetypes.blurb: a mob/boss casts something with a kickable bar.
archetypes.tell: a visible cast bar on a spell you can kick (Polymorph, a heal, a bolt, a summon).
archetypes.response: interrupt it. Rotate kicks/stuns across the group; the worst casts (heals, summons, raidwide channels) come first.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵 usually (one cast leaks) → 🔴 if it's a heal/summon/empower (e.g. Reanimation, Healing Touch, Arcing Mana).
archetypes.role: all — every DPS/tank/healer with a kick. The non-negotiable group job.
archetypes.examples: Healing Touch — Overgrown Ancient (Algeth'ar Academy); Reanimation — Reanimated Warrior trash (Maisara Caverns).
archetypes.diagramIdea: mob silhouette with a cast bar mid-fill and a red "X" kick icon snapping it shut.

archetypes.name: Ground Void Zone
archetypes.blurb: a damaging puddle/patch you stand out of (and that often grows or shrinks the room).
archetypes.tell: a colored circle, swirl, or spreading goo appears under or near you.
archetypes.response: move out / don't stand in it. Drop boss-placed zones at the edge so you don't eat the arena.
archetypes.consequence: 🟠 standing in it kills you fast → 🔴 when zones stack and eat the floor (Leyline Array, Core Exposure).
archetypes.examples: Flamestrike — Blazing Pyromancer trash (Magisters' Terrace); Coalesced Wind — Ranjit (Skyreach).
archetypes.diagramIdea: top-down floor with a glowing puddle and a footstep arrow stepping clear of the edge.

archetypes.name: Tank Buster
archetypes.blurb: a big melee hit, stacking shred, or bleed aimed at whoever holds threat.
archetypes.tell: boss/elite winds up a heavy melee swing or applies a stacking armor/heal-absorb debuff on the tank.
archetypes.response: tank presses an active mitigation cooldown; healer tops the tank; trade taunts if stacks get scary.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵 mostly (mitigate it) → 🟠/🔴 unmitigated or with stacked shred/absorb (Hulking Fragment, Blight Smash).
archetypes.examples: Orebreaker — Forgemaster Garfrost (Pit of Saron); Crunch Armor (stacking shred) — Bramblemaw Bear trash (Maisara Caverns).
archetypes.diagramIdea: side view of a shield icon flashing "MIT" as a big fist lands on the tank.

archetypes.name: Spread Out
archetypes.blurb: damage/debuff that hits an area around its target, so clumping chains it.
archetypes.tell: a marker on you (or several players) that will detonate, or spawns a patch where you stand.
archetypes.response: spread to your assigned spacing before it lands; don't share your splash with melee.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵→🟠 (you eat your own) → 🔴 if overlap chains the group (Runic Mark, Plague Expulsion).
archetypes.examples: Runic Mark — Seranel Sunlash (Magisters' Terrace); Mana Bombs — Vexamus (Algeth'ar Academy).
archetypes.diagramIdea: five dots fanning outward from a clumped cluster, arrows pushing apart.

archetypes.name: Dispel
archetypes.blurb: a debuff on a player (or buff to remove) that someone must cleanse off.
archetypes.tell: a harmful magic/curse/poison/disease icon on a player, a heal-absorb shield, or an offensive buff on a friendly-turned-add.
archetypes.response: dispel the flagged target (or free the shackled ally). Know which school your class can remove.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵 usually → 🟠/🔴 when the debuff is lethal or feeds the boss (Burning Radiance, Curse of Darkness).
archetypes.role: all dispellers — frequently the healer's signature job, sometimes any class with a tradeable cleanse.
archetypes.examples: Ethereal Shackles — Arcane Sentry trash (Magisters' Terrace); Lasher Toxin — Overgrown Ancient (Algeth'ar Academy).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a debuff icon over a player head with a green sparkle wiping it away.

archetypes.name: Kill-Priority Add
archetypes.blurb: a spawned/secondary unit that must die (or be CC'd) before it does something bad.
archetypes.tell: an add appears — a totem, a caster, an orb, a "Voidcaller" — usually with its own cast or a buff it feeds.
archetypes.response: swap and burn it (or stun/interrupt) on the timer. Treat the add, not the boss, as the threat.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵→🟠 (a single add leaks) → 🔴 when ignored adds empower or overwhelm (Mana Battery, Army of the Dead).
archetypes.role: all — DPS swaps, tank grabs, everyone helps cleave.
archetypes.examples: Army of the Dead — Scourgelord Tyrannus (Pit of Saron); Mana Battery (Corespark Overload) — Flux Engineer trash (Nexus-Point Xenas).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a small add glowing with a skull/priority marker while the boss is greyed out.

archetypes.name: Frontal Cone
archetypes.blurb: a directional sweep/breath/cleave in front of the caster.
archetypes.tell: boss/mob faces a direction and winds up a cone, breath, or wide cleave (often a tank-facing arrow).
archetypes.response: get out of the front; tank points it away from the group; melee sidestep behind.
archetypes.consequence: 🟠 mostly → 🔵 when small/cleave → 🔴 on the big ones (Wind Chakram).
archetypes.examples: Fire Breath — Emberdawn (Windrunner Spire); Shield Slam (frontal) — Runed Spellbreaker trash (Magisters' Terrace).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a wedge fanning from the mob's face with a player dodging to the flank.

archetypes.name: Knockback
archetypes.blurb: a shove, pull, or repel that moves you (often toward a hazard or off a ledge).
archetypes.tell: a slam/gale/vacuum wind-up, sometimes a pull-in or bouncing orbs.
archetypes.response: position with your back safe before it fires; use anti-knockback if you have it; pre-stack against pulls.
archetypes.consequence: 🟠 (knocked into the void/hazard) → 🔵 when harmless reposition → 🔴 on arena-ledge fights (Collapsing Void, Burning Gale).
archetypes.examples: Burning Gale — Emberdawn (Windrunner Spire); Crowd Dispersal — Arcane Sentry trash (Magisters' Terrace).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a player figure blown along a motion-arc toward a void edge, with a "brace" anchor icon.

archetypes.name: Soak
archetypes.blurb: a mechanic that demands bodies in it: stand in the orb/zone/beam to absorb or split it.
archetypes.tell: an orb to grab, an energy bar refueling, a beam/bomb that needs N players, or a stabilize cast.
archetypes.response: assign soakers and get in. The failure here is under-soaking, the opposite of a void zone.
archetypes.consequence: 🔴 mostly — missed soaks wipe (Refueling Protocol, Wave of Silence, Overload, Energize).
archetypes.examples: Refueling Protocol / Energy Orb — Arcanotron Custos (Magisters' Terrace); Stabilize — Rift Warden trash (Seat of the Triumvirate).
archetypes.diagramIdea: glowing orb with two player figures stepping into it (green check), one staying out (red X).

archetypes.name: Raid Damage
archetypes.blurb: unavoidable group-wide pulse/channel (and incidental single-target spikes the healer must catch).
archetypes.tell: a raidwide cast, a screech/nova, a stacking room-wide DoT, or a sudden spike on one player.
archetypes.response: pre-heal and use raid cooldowns on the channel; healer triages the spikes. Often paired with an interrupt window.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵 (healable) → 🟠/🔴 when stacked or untended (Bone Infusion, Supernova, Burst Forth).
archetypes.examples: Supernova — Araknath (Skyreach); Spore Dispersal — Bloated Lasher trash (Windrunner Spire).
archetypes.diagramIdea: the whole party ringed by an expanding shockwave with healer cooldown sparkles overhead.

archetypes.name: Stack Up
archetypes.blurb: damage/debuff that wants the group together to split or share it.
archetypes.tell: a marker that splits damage by player count, a shared-soak debuff, or a melee-stack cue.
archetypes.response: collapse to the stack point on time (the inverse of spread-out).
archetypes.consequence: 🟠 (solo-eaten) → 🔴 when the whole group must share (Void Destruction, Arcane Volley).
archetypes.examples: Arcane Volley — Animated Codex trash (Magisters' Terrace); Void Destruction — Degentrius (Magisters' Terrace).
archetypes.diagramIdea: scattered dots converging onto one bracketed stack marker.

archetypes.name: Fixate / Chase
archetypes.blurb: an enemy locks onto one player and pursues, ignoring threat.
archetypes.tell: a mob (or boss) targets a non-tank and runs them down; sometimes a tornado/blades chase.
archetypes.response: the fixated player kites with speed/CC until it drops; don't drag it through the group.
archetypes.consequence: 🟠 mostly (caught = dead) → 🔵 on slow chases → 🔴 if it bodies the group (Bladestorm, Flailstorm).
archetypes.role: all (whoever's tagged kites; tank can't taunt it off).
archetypes.examples: Burning Pursuit — Rukhran (Skyreach); Fixate — Radiant Swarm trash (Nexus-Point Xenas).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a mob with an eye icon trailing a fleeing player along a kite path.

archetypes.name: Purge / Soothe
archetypes.blurb: an enemy buff or enrage you remove from the mob (not a player debuff).
archetypes.tell: a mob gains haste/damage/enrage, a fortifying buff, or a frenzy you can soothe/dispel off it.
archetypes.response: purge/soothe the buff off the enemy (or kill the buff source) before it snowballs.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵 mostly → 🟠/🔴 on enrages and pet-cleave windows (Bestial Wrath, Plague Frenzy). (The Derelict Duo's Broken Bond reads like an enrage but is not soothable — see balance-kill.)
archetypes.examples: Bolstering Flames — Territorial Dragonhawk trash (Windrunner Spire); Plague Frenzy (soothe) — Lumbering Plaguehorror trash (Pit of Saron).
archetypes.diagramIdea: an angry red buff icon over a mob being stripped to grey.

archetypes.name: Positional Gimmick
archetypes.blurb: the mechanic is solved by where you stand: line-of-sight, reflect angle, or move-the-mob.
archetypes.tell: a beam/quills cast you must LoS behind a pillar, a reflect-shield you must hit from a side, or an add you must reposition.
archetypes.response: break line of sight, attack from the correct facing, or drag the mob to the marked spot.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵→🔴 depending (Searing Quills / Glacial Overload are LoS-or-wipe).
archetypes.examples: Searing Quills (line-of-sight) — Rukhran (Skyreach); Vigilant Defense (reflect, hit from behind) — Bound Defender trash (Maisara Caverns).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a pillar between player and boss-beam, with a dotted "blocked" sightline.

archetypes.name: Heal Absorb
archetypes.blurb: a shield/debuff that soaks incoming healing until it's burned off.
archetypes.tell: a dark shield bar over a player's health; heals fill it instead of the health bar.
archetypes.response: burn the absorb down (overheal or DPS the shield); don't waste cooldowns trying to out-heal a full absorb.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵→🟠 (the player can't be healed until cleared, then dies to follow-up).
archetypes.role: healer-critical (DPS may need to attack the shield).
archetypes.examples: Null Sunder — Shadowguard Defender trash (Nexus-Point Xenas); Entropic Leech — Duskfright Herald trash (Nexus-Point Xenas).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a health bar with a black overlay shield, healing arrows bouncing off it.

archetypes.name: Charge
archetypes.blurb: a fast gap-closer the mob slams into a target's position.
archetypes.tell: a mob breaks formation and rushes a player (intercept/charging slash/break ranks).
archetypes.response: sidestep the line; tank picks it back up; don't let it land on the backline.
archetypes.consequence: ⚪→🟠 (most are minor, the breakers hurt — Break Ranks).
archetypes.examples: Break Ranks — Phalanx Breaker trash (Windrunner Spire); Intercepting Charge — Haunting Grunt trash (Windrunner Spire).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a dashed charge line from mob to player with a sidestep arrow off it.

archetypes.name: Proximity Bait
archetypes.blurb: a mechanic baited by closeness: get near (or away) to place/trigger it correctly.
archetypes.tell: a cast that targets the nearest player, or a root/zone dropped where someone is standing.
archetypes.response: the designated baiter steps to the safe drop spot; everyone else stays clear of the trigger radius.
archetypes.consequence: 🟠 (caught in the placement) — usually a death, not a wipe.
archetypes.examples: Rend Souls (proximity bait) — Hollow Soulrender trash (Maisara Caverns); Cries of the Fallen (proximity bait root) — Rak'tul, Vessel of Souls (Maisara Caverns).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a player stepping to a marked drop-spot with a radius ring pulling the cast toward them.

archetypes.name: Pulsing Aura
archetypes.blurb: a persistent, location-less damage/chill aura that just ticks while you're near the source.
archetypes.tell: no dodgeable shape — a stacking chill/dread debuff that ramps the longer the pack lives.
archetypes.response: burn the source fast; healer rides the ramp; spread if the aura also debuffs proximity.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵→🟠 (it ramps — kill the caster before it overwhelms).
archetypes.examples: Siphoning Chill — Forgemaster Garfrost (Pit of Saron); Dread Pulse — Dreadpulse Lich trash (Pit of Saron).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a mob radiating concentric faint rings with a rising debuff-stack counter.

archetypes.name: Burn Window
archetypes.blurb: a phase where the boss becomes vulnerable and you dump damage cooldowns; the "mechanic" is capitalizing, not dodging.
archetypes.tell: the boss takes massively increased damage (a shield drops, an intermission opens, all the adds/notes are cleared, an execute/sub-threshold phase begins).
archetypes.response: pool and pop DPS cooldowns / Bloodlust for the window; maximize burst while it's open. Failing it doesn't kill you — it overruns the timer and snowballs the next cycle.
archetypes.consequence: 🔵 (your job to capitalize) → 🔴 indirectly when a missed window means the fight never ends and a later mechanic wipes you.
archetypes.examples: Siphon Void (after all 6 Notes silenced) — L'ura (Seat of the Triumvirate).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a boss health bar flashing "+DMG" with cooldown icons lighting up beneath it.

archetypes.name: Balance Kill (Paired Bosses)
archetypes.blurb: two linked enemies that must die together; leaving one alive triggers a runaway enrage on the survivor.
archetypes.tell: two bosses sharing the fight, with a stacking enrage/empower on whichever one outlives the other.
archetypes.response: keep both at even HP and cleave them down to die simultaneously; don't tunnel one. Not interruptible, dispellable, or soothable — it's a damage-routing problem.
archetypes.consequence: 🔴 — a lopsided kill enrages the survivor and wipes the group.
archetypes.role: all (DPS balance their target damage; tank keeps both together).
archetypes.examples: Broken Bond — Derelict Duo / Latch & Kalis (Windrunner Spire).
archetypes.diagramIdea: two health bars side by side with a balance-scale icon and a warning when they diverge.

archetypes.name: Flavor
archetypes.blurb: low-priority filler: chip damage, cosmetic casts, defensives that don't demand a reaction.
archetypes.tell: a small auto-ish hit, a self-buff like Shield Wall, or a tiny poke with no real consequence.
archetypes.response: nothing special — keep doing your job. Listed so the trainer can mark "safe to ignore."
archetypes.consequence: ⚪ flavor.
archetypes.examples: Shield Wall — Commander Kroluk (Windrunner Spire); Shoot — Swiftshot Archer trash (Windrunner Spire).
archetypes.diagramIdea: a greyed-out ability icon with a small "ignore" tag.

abilities.whatItDoes: AoE hit + knocks all players back, drops a puddle (Arcane Residue) under the boss for 2 min
abilities.response: Pre-heal; drop these along the room edge or overlapping existing puddles to save space

abilities.whatItDoes: Heavy-damage pool left by Expulsion / Refueling
abilities.response: Don't stand in it; tank moves boss off it for melee uptime

abilities.whatItDoes: Roots 2 random players + periodic damage
abilities.response: Magic dispel / freedom it; immunity or self-dispel (e.g. Stoneform) works; defensive if it overlaps Expulsion

abilities.whatItDoes: Heavy arcane hit + knockback on the tank
abilities.response: Tank defensive; use terrain to stop the knockback

abilities.response: Burn cooldowns; loosely spread to soak Energy Orbs

abilities.response: Soak every Orb so none reach the boss

abilities.whatItDoes: Stacking DoT from soaking Orbs
abilities.response: Watch stacks; defensive if you soak several

abilities.whatItDoes: Boss drops a zone under itself; standing in it triggers Feedback (clears Runic Mark) but silences/pacifies
abilities.response: Place near arena center; use it to clear Runic Mark and to survive Wave of Silence

abilities.whatItDoes: Debuff inflicting moderate damage to nearby players
abilities.response: Step into the Suppression Zone to clear it; 2 marked players leave a gap so Null Reaction hits don't combo the group

abilities.whatItDoes: Stacking debuff applied when clearing Runic Mark; makes dodging harder
abilities.response: The 2nd player to clear may need a defensive; keep gaps to avoid combos

abilities.whatItDoes: Effect inside Suppression Zone that clears Runic Mark but silences/pacifies
abilities.response: Use deliberately to clear marks / survive Wave of Silence

abilities.whatItDoes: At 100 energy: heavy damage + 8s silence/pacify to players outside a Suppression Zone
abilities.response: Step into the Suppression Zone just before the cast finishes

abilities.whatItDoes: Buff: increases Seranel's attack speed + adds Arcane to melee
abilities.response: Purge it; otherwise tank uses a major defensive

abilities.whatItDoes: At fight start and 50% HP, creates clones that drift around; all share health via Synaptic Nexus
abilities.response: Cleave the clones — shared HP. Below 50% every player incl. tank is targeted by a clone

abilities.whatItDoes: Heavy damage, leaves a Void Secretions puddle under the target
abilities.response: Drop the puddle out of the way; reposition off Void Secretions

abilities.whatItDoes: The pool spawned by Cosmic Sting
abilities.response: Don't stand in it; move out promptly

abilities.whatItDoes: Debuff linking you to a specific clone (arrow under your feet)
abilities.response: Run to and touch the linked clone to clear the debuff and strip the boss's shield

abilities.whatItDoes: Pulls players toward their Gemellus; pull-in circles appear around all clones
abilities.response: Run away from the pull-in; avoid the Cosmic Radiation aura in the boss's hit range

abilities.whatItDoes: Aura within hit range of the boss during Astral Grasp
abilities.response: Use movement speed to escape; healer spot-heals those caught

abilities.whatItDoes: Beam(s) that carve the arena into "pizza-slice" zones; contact = moderate damage + stun
abilities.response: Never touch the beams; keep group split across both sides

abilities.whatItDoes: Bouncing ball that must be soaked; if no one is under it on impact, raid-wide damage
abilities.response: Spread to cover each slice so someone always catches it

abilities.whatItDoes: Stackable raid debuff applied when an Unstable Void Essence is missed
abilities.response: Don't miss soaks — >1 stack usually wipes the group

abilities.whatItDoes: Tank-targeted hit that can one-shot nearby players, applies Umbral Splinters Magic DoT
abilities.response: Tank avoids clipping the party; back away, get dispelled; drops Stygian Ichor out of melee

abilities.whatItDoes: Magic DoT on the tank that leaves a hard-to-walk ichor pool
abilities.response: Healer dispels; tank drops the ichor away from where DPS stand

abilities.whatItDoes: Debuffs random players with varying durations; creates Entropy Orbs on removal
abilities.response: Defensive if your timer is long; aim orbs where others can see them; healer major CDs

abilities.whatItDoes: Boss's ranged attack used regardless of your position (it never melees)
abilities.response: Keep healing through it; no melee to dodge

abilities.whatItDoes: Tank mechanic: massive raid damage if not triggered by a player
abilities.response: Trigger it (tank) so it doesn't go off untouched

reveal.response: If targeted, stand still + use a defensive (or combat-drop) to break the channel; healer spot-heals

reveal.response: Tank active mitigation; put your back to an arrow to cancel the knockback (also clears Squall Leap)

reveal.response: Tank grabs threat; rotate poison dispels; stun/slow/knockback to kite.

reveal.response: Active mitigation; very dangerous if any Lashers are alive.

reveal.response: Swap to it, interrupt Healing Touch; tank taunts on spawn.

reveal.response: Interrupt at all costs.

reveal.response: Stand in the Abundance circle (8 yd) to cleanse Splinterbark; time the Branch death when Lashers are near dead so they don't get healed.

reveal.response: Active mitigation every cast.

reveal.whatItDoes: Frontal at a random target that knocks players away.
reveal.response: Sidestep out of the cone.

reveal.whatItDoes: Interrupts/silences casters + party-wide DoT; splash if stacked.
reveal.response: Stay loosely spread; group defensive / healing CD.

reveal.whatItDoes: At 75%/45% Crawth channels Ruinous Winds; only interruptible by scoring 3 balls in a goal.
reveal.response: Grab 3 balls, throw into a goal to cancel the cast.

cue.spell: Goal of the Rushing Winds → Gale Force

reveal.whatItDoes: Scoring the air goal spawns roaming Roving Cyclones + pushback winds for the rest of the fight (grants Haste).
reveal.response: Recommended first goal (75%); dodge the patrolling cyclones.

cue.spell: Goal of the Searing Blaze → Firestorm

reveal.whatItDoes: Scoring the fire goal gives Crawth +75% damage taken for 12s, then permanent Blistering Fire pulsing AoE + fire circles.
reveal.response: Recommended at 45%; pop Bloodlust/CDs into the damage amp; avoid fire pools.

reveal.response: Spread out and soak orbs before they reach Vexamus; hard to take >1 without an immunity.

reveal.whatItDoes: Heavy frontal cone on the tank.
reveal.response: Tank points it away from the group; defensive.

reveal.response: Defensive; drop pools toward the room edge out of the way.

reveal.response: Healing CD for the hit; brace the knockback; sidestep your circles.

reveal.whatItDoes: On pull / 100 energy, spawns 2 Arcane Rifts.
reveal.response: Be topped before pull; tank her in a corner toward the entrance.

reveal.whatItDoes: Every hit stacks the buff; at 3 stacks it drops an Arcane Rift under you and resets.
reveal.response: Keep a couple stacks for Haste, but don't greed to 3 in a bad spot; place rifts well.

reveal.whatItDoes: Rifts periodically fire Uncontrolled Energy orbs across the floor.
reveal.response: Dodge the orbs; keep rifts in tidy positions.

reveal.whatItDoes: Lands on a random player.
reveal.response: Personal defensive; loosely spread to avoid splashing allies.

reveal.whatItDoes: Random-target nuke; can combo with Energy Bomb.
reveal.response: Defensive if it overlaps Energy Bomb; healer spot-heals.

reveal.whatItDoes: Pulls all players to the boss + AoE.
reveal.response: Run out / use movement; tank positions her so the pull doesn't land players on a rift.

reveal.whatItDoes: Heavy arcane tank hit; applies an Overwhelming Power stack.
reveal.response: Active mitigation; if already at 2 stacks, stand somewhere safe to drop the resulting rift.

reveal.whatItDoes: Drops a swirly under each player
reveal.response: step out (disorients if hit). CC-immune.

reveal.whatItDoes: Stacking tank hit
reveal.response: tank uses defensives / bleed cleanse on stacks.

reveal.whatItDoes: Most dangerous trash here. Darting Sting instant-charges a random player; stacking Agitation enrage buffs its damage
reveal.response: Soothe/enrage-dispel, blow cooldowns, stay near pack to cleave.

reveal.whatItDoes: Hits the tank hard

reveal.whatItDoes: On the tank, stacking bleed

reveal.whatItDoes: Frontal at a random target
reveal.response: sidestep out. CC-immune.

reveal.response: All players avoid the puddle under the boss

reveal.whatItDoes: Dead Sunwings leave an egg; a new Sunwing dying on top re-hatches it

reveal.whatItDoes: Heavy fire damage to everyone in line of sight across the platform

reveal.whatItDoes: Cast if no one is in melee range for too long

reveal.whatItDoes: Focuses a player and burns anything in the beam's path, leaving Blazing Ground

reveal.whatItDoes: DoT on 3 players at a time
reveal.response: Defensive if hit repeatedly; healer watches the 3 targets
